_comment: Toggle each matching channel on/off and tune its threshold. Re-run 'just run_main' after editing. Disabled channels are skipped entirely.

name.description: Cosine similarity between the embedding of an extracted function call name and KB concept short names.

summary.description: Cosine similarity between the file comment block embedding and KB concept docstring summaries.

title.description: Cosine similarity between the notebook title (first comment heading) and KB concept summaries.

pattern_desc.description: Cosine similarity between the file comment block and the full pattern intent+context+solution text.

defined_doc.description: Cosine similarity between concatenated docstrings of classes/functions DEFINED in the file (not just called) and KB concept summaries. Targets library-style files where call sites use generic primitives but the implementing class docstring describes the algorithm.

internal_keywords.description: Each KB concept carries a string of composed (snake_case/CamelCase) tokens harvested from its method bodies (parameter names, return-type annotations, local variable names) after stopword filtering. For every target call-site name we compute cosine similarity against each concept's keyword vector. Skipped for concepts that have no internal_keywords.

internal_comments.description: Each KB concept carries the inline `#` comments found inside its method bodies (NOT the docstring). The target file's comment block is compared against each concept's comment vector. Skipped for concepts that have no internal_comments.

keyword_veto.description: Post-match veto: drop matches from semantic text channels (summary, title, defined_doc, pattern_desc) when no exclusive keyword for the matched pattern appears anywhere in the file text. Exclusive keywords are auto-generated from KB concept text by scripts/extract_pattern_keywords.py — words that appear in at most 2 patterns, making them discriminating rather than generic.
